content:
I built and installed 6.9 kernel with Rust support. Steps:

  1. I'm using Ubuntu server 24.04 VM (ARM) in Parallels desktop (MacOS, M3)
  2. Cloned rust-for-linux repo (tag rust-6.9)

  3. Compiled and installed kernel:
    make clean && make mrproper
    LLVM=1 make olddefconfig
    LLVM=1 make menuconfig - Enabled Rust (samples, rust hacking)
    LLVM=1 make rustavailable - Rust is available!
    LLVM=1 make rust-analyzer
    LLVM=1 make -j$(nproc)
    LLVM=1 make modules_install
    LLVM=1 make install

  4. Reboot (new kernel is running)

My question:
How can I build a simple rust kernel module which would use Rust code together with C?
I want to be able to call C functions from Rust code in my project - what's the properly way to generate C bindings?
How to configure my Rust project properly in this case? Do I need Cargo.toml or just Kconfig/Makefile?
How to let my project know about kernel which is in <linux_source>/rust? (not accessible in any .rs file within my project)

I did my research and what I found - is simple out-of-tree kernel modules without C code (examples).

What I want to try is to call C functions from Rust code within this out-of-tree kernel module (I suppose I need build.rs?).
https://github.com/OpenVPN/ovpn-dco

I'm seeking for any examples - out of tree kernel module written in C + Rust, where C code is being called from Rust code
In case you have not seen these yet, please take a look at https://rust-for-linux.com/out-of-tree-modules and https://docs.kernel.org/rust/general-information.html.

Miguel Ojeda said:

In case you have not seen these yet, please take a look at https://rust-for-linux.com/out-of-tree-modules and https://docs.kernel.org/rust/general-information.html.

Still not that clear - I tried to follow this guide, but he was coding inside samples/rust directory. Do I need to do the same? Or how to tell my project about kernel crate? Should I specify dependency it in build.rs/Cargo.toml
https://youtu.be/-l-8WrGHEGI?si=UEuGueT1P6LJgt9t
Please read the official documentation first -- for instance, your question about whether to use Cargo is answered there.

In the links I gave you, there is also a template you can use to get started. Having said that, I would recommend doing your development in-tree if you are starting. It is simpler and better supported.
Nikolai Shv has marked this topic as resolved.
